exemplar.title: â To what extent does fashion industry influenced the practices of wearing hijab among Muslim women in Malaysia? Does it align with Islamic teachings and guidelines?

detailedExemplarData.title: â To what extent does fashion industry influenced the practices of wearing hijab among Muslim women in Malaysia? Does it align with Islamic teachings and guidelines?

strengths: The topic is communicated accurately with sufficient social-cultural context and revisited throughout the report.
The section â4.1 HIJAB AND FASHION IN MALAYSIAâ effectively refocuses discussion on the local context.
The survey scope and demographic rationale are well explained, demonstrating thoughtful research design.
The research question is clearly stated on the title page and remains central to the discussion.
weaknesses: The title lacks specificity regarding hijab practice versus broader fashion industry focus.
The research question combines two inquiries and contains a tense error, reducing precision.
Justification for sample representativeness and demographic bias is omitted in the methodology.
Occasional repetition and minor language errors reduce overall clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place communication in band 3â4 (score 4), the research question in lower band 3â4 (score 3), and methodology likewise in band 3â4 (score 3). Overall, the work consistently meets the descriptors for adequate, sustained communication and a mostly complete method, justifying a final Focus and Method score of 4.

strengths: Use of Qurâanic verses (e.g., direct quote of Surah An-Nur 24:31) provides a strong primary-text basis.
Key terminology (hijab, aurat, fiqh, Shariah) is used correctly and consistently.
Effective synthesis of scriptural guidance and societal implications in concluding verse discussions.
Conversion of qualitative responses to numerical data enhances methodological transparency.
weaknesses: Discussion remains largely descriptive; some conflation of etymologies and revealed versus empirical knowledge.
Heavy reliance on a general web source for the hijab definition weakens academic authority.
Inconsistent citation formatting and incomplete bibliographic details in places.
Minor spelling/grammar slips (e.g., âquidelinesâ, âSvariahâ) reduce polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Subject knowledge, source use, and terminology each fall in the 3â4 band (strands scored at mid-band 3). The work shows good understanding and generally relevant application of sources, with mostly accurate terminology. While not thorough or critically nuanced in all areas, it justifies an upper-band score of 4 for Knowledge and Understanding.

strengths: Survey analysis is clearly linked to the research question and presented with bar charts that reinforce textual interpretation.
Use of a practical brand example (Naelofar) provides strong real-world evidence.
The conclusion synthesizes survey insights well, connecting fashion influence with religious adherence.
Critical reflection on survey limitations (bias, sample size) demonstrates evaluative intent.
weaknesses: Overall argument structure remains descriptive and lacks a systematically developed line of reasoning.
Overemphasis on mean values oversimplifies data without measures of spread or confidence.
Evaluation of how fashion aligns with Islamic teaching is brief and sometimes asserted rather than fully evidenced.
Limited engagement with alternative scholarly perspectives or deeper counterarguments.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research analysis and evaluation both sit in the 4â6 band (strands scored 4), while argumentation is in the 1â3 band (strand scored 2). The combined performance falls between the solid mid-band and the lower end of the 7â9 band, warranting a Critical Thinking score of 7 to reflect good relevance and emerging depth, though not yet consistently thorough.

strengths: Clear structure with chapters, sub-sections, and a contents page supports navigation.
Title page, word count, and page numbers are correctly applied.
Figures are appropriately placed and generally well formatted.
Section headings (e.g., â2.0 DEFINITION OF HIJABâ) effectively guide the reader.
weaknesses: Table of contents lacks page numbers for most sections, hampering navigation.
Inconsistent numbering (e.g., âFigure 1â used twice, mixed bullet numbering) creates minor confusion.
Formatting inconsistencies in font size and caption styles reduce cohesion.
Inline math formatting for verse citations is inappropriate; should be plain text.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both structure and layout strands meet descriptors for the 3â4 band at the lower end. Organization and layout support understanding overall, but minor inconsistencies prevent full marks, justifying a Presentation score of 3.

strandSelections.reasoning: The argument follows the essay structure but is mainly descriptive, moving from definitions to survey data without a fully developed line of reasoning.  Links between fashion trends, scriptural mandates and quantitative results are suggested rather than systematically built.  This corresponds to an argument that shows "some structure but lacks clarity," meriting 2 in the 1-3 band.

Twenty-five sources are listed, covering academic articles, Qurâan websites and popular media; citations appear in-text though formatting is erratic.  Material chosen is generally relevant, yet integration is uneven and critical comparison is limited.  Adequate application places the work comfortably in the 3-4 band at 3.

A basic methodology (200-respondent Google-Form survey plus literature review) is described, with details of question format and simple statistical treatment (means).  Source selection and data collection are broadly relevant, but sampling limitations and analytic tools are only briefly justified.  This constitutes a mostly complete though not sophisticated method, meriting 3 within the 3-4 band.

The topicâinteraction between hijab practice and fashion in Malaysiaâis stated early, placed in a clear sociocultural context and revisited throughout.  Background to social-media influence, local brands and Islamic requirements is supplied, giving the reader sufficient orientation.  Minor language errors and occasional repetition reduce precision, yet overall communication remains adequate and sustained, fitting the 3-4 band; the stronger framing at the start justifies the upper value of 4.

The essay includes a title page, word count, page numbers and appropriately placed figures.  Table formatting is basic yet functional.  Minor inconsistencies in font size and occasional mis-aligned captions prevent full marks, but layout "is fully present and correctly applied" overall, warranting 3.

Survey results are tabulated and converted into simple means; bar charts are produced and linked back to each factor.  The analysis demonstrates a clear, though surface-level, relevance to the research question.  Limited statistical depth keeps it from the 7-9 band, but it exceeds the basic adequacy of 1-3, so 4 (lower end of 4-6 band) is justified.

Terminology such as hijab, aurat, fiqh and Shariah is used correctly and repeatedly.  Occasional spelling/grammatical slips ("quidelines", "Svariah") show inconsistency, but accuracy is mostly maintained.  Hence the strand meets the descriptor for 3-4; a middle score of 3 is appropriate.

The conclusion summarises findings and notes survey limitations; some critical reflection on bias and sample size is present.  However, evaluation of how fashion aligns with Islamic teaching remains brief and sometimes asserted rather than evidenced.  Quality fits the 4-6 band, with a cautious score of 4.

Chapters, sub-sections and a contents page make the structure easy to follow, and headings correspond to methodological stages.  Some duplication (two "Figure 1" labels) and abrupt transitions weaken cohesion, but overall organisation supports reader understanding.  This achieves the lower end of the 3-4 band.

The research question is clearly written and located on the title page, but it combines two separate inquiries and contains a tense error ("influenced").  Focus is acceptableâlimited to Muslim women in Malaysiaâyet the double scope makes it only partially focused.  This meets the descriptor for 3-4; a mid-band mark of 3 reflects the partial focus.

The student shows solid knowledge of Qurâanic verses, hadiths, and Malaysian hijab history, explaining key concepts like aurat and mahram.  Discussion remains largely descriptive and occasionally confused (e.g., conflating Persian/Arabic etymology), so depth is good but not thorough.  This aligns with the middle of the 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: The title is thematic but lacks specificity regarding the focus on hijab practice and the fashion industry. Consider a more descriptive title that directly reflects the research scope.
Word count is provided at the start and remains under the 4000-word limit, meeting formatting requirements.
A stray mathematical notation $\overline{2}$ appears in the introduction without context; remove or clarify this to prevent reader confusion.
Reiterating the research question verbatim in the purpose section reduces writing sophistication. Paraphrase to avoid repetition and improve academic tone.
The research question uses incorrect tense and combines two inquiries. Revise to a single clear question using correct verb form (e.g., âTo what extent does the fashion industry influenceâ¦â).
The table of contents lacks page numbers for most sections, making navigation difficult. Add page references beside each heading.
Page numbers appear consistently in the footer, facilitating orientation through the document.
The opening paragraph describes social media exposure but delays stating the research focus. Introduce the topicâs relevance earlier for clearer framing.
The etymological discussion on turban origins conflates contexts; verify source accuracy or streamline to maintain clarity.
The clear subsection heading â2.0 DEFINITION OF HIJABâ effectively guides the reader into definitions.
The subsection â4.1 HIJAB AND FASHION IN MALAYSIAâ effectively refocuses the discussion on the local context.
Page numbering remains consistent, aiding reader orientation.
Incorporating the Hadith on conveying from the Prophet effectively links tradition with contemporary practice.
The discussion of Nagli conflates revealed and empirical knowledge; clarify terminology and relevance to hijab practices.
Footnote formatting remains inconsistent; remove HTML relics and ensure clear presentation of sources.
List items use inconsistent numbering (âLâ, âIIâ, âIIIâ); standardize enumeration for clarity.
Explaining the conversion of qualitative responses to numerical data enhances methodological transparency.
Score ranges â5 â 9â, â10 â 14â, â15 â 20â lack linkage to total question score; clarify the relation.
Caption duplication (âFigure 1â used twice) causes confusion; update the second chartâs numbering.
Reporting only the mean simplifies data; include measures of spread or confidence to enrich analysis.
The hijab definition relies heavily on a general web source. Strengthen with scholarly or primary religious definitions to deepen subject authority.
Quoting Surah An-Nur (24:31) directly provides a strong primary-text basis for Islamic guidelines.
The verse citation appears formatted as inline math; reformat the reference â(An-Nur 24:31)â as plain text for consistency.
The synthesis of commentary by Ibn Kathir and Salmani could be deepened by contrasting alternate scholarly views.
The student effectively synthesizes scriptural guidance and societal implications in concluding the verse discussion.
The historical narrative on Imam Sirinâs demonstration lacks a source citation; provide a reference to validate the account.
Defining fashion in both secular and Islamic terms provides clear conceptual grounding for subsequent analysis.
The scoring cut-off ranges lack justification in relation to question counts; explain why these thresholds were chosen.
To strengthen evaluation, compare multiple tafsir sources instead of a single commentary when interpreting scriptural directives.
The section header â3.0 ISLAMIC GUIDELINES OF HIJAB FOR WOMANâ clearly demarcates content, improving overall structure.
Footnote HTML artifacts (â<sup>â) appear in the note; clean up formatting to maintain professional presentation.
The hadith quoted from At-Thirmidhi should be accompanied by an assessment of its isnad to ensure authenticity.
Footnote HTML remnants (â<sup>â) appear; revise these to plain text for consistent formatting.
The citation for SU-LYN (2015) is incomplete in-text; include full bibliographic details when first mentioned.
Caption âHijab in 1900âsâ is vague; specify the exact decade or year for clearer historical context.
Linking the Naelofar brand example back to the research question provides strong practical evidence.
The final discussion raises balancing fashion and faith but would benefit from references to literature on identity negotiation.
The concluding summary sentence on institutional influence is abrupt; add a transition to link with methodology.
The methodology description omits justification for sample representativeness; discuss demographic bias and sampling limitations.
The methodology section heading is clearly marked, supporting reader navigation.
The survey scope and demographic rationale are well explained, demonstrating thoughtful research design.
Section header â7.1 ISLAMIC GUIDELINES TOWARDS HIJABâ is clearly formatted, guiding the reader.
Figure 1 effectively visualizes knowledge distribution and supports the textual analysis.
Figure 2âs visual presentation is clear, illustrating variation in fashion influence perceptibly.
Caption incorrectly labels this chart as Figure 1; renumber accurately to Figure 2 for consistency.
Discuss the possible reasons behind 35% perceiving strong influence to deepen critical engagement.
Figure 3 visually aligns data with textual interpretation, reinforcing argument coherence.
The figure heading uses a top-level heading tag; adjust to match figure caption style for consistency.
The conclusion synthesizes survey insights well, connecting fashion influence with religious adherence.
Overemphasis on the mean oversimplifies data; acknowledge limitations of summarizing with a single measure.
The page number appears consistently in the footer, maintaining navigational clarity.
Future research suggestion is strong; specifying qualitative interview frameworks would deepen methodological guidance.
